name: 100 Percent Whole-Wheat Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir yeast and sugar into water; let stand until foamy, about 5 minutes.
2. In a food processor fitted with a metal blade or in a bowl mixed with a dough hook, combine flour, milk powder, oil, and egg or egg whites.
3. Turn the machine on and combine the mixture. With the machine running, slowly add the yeast mixture. Mix until the dough cleans the sides of the bowl.
4. If the dough is too sticky, add more flour by the tablespoon, working it in before adding more. If the dough is crumbly and dry, add more water by the teaspoon, working it in before adding more.
5. Once the desired consistency is reached (moist but not sticky), mix the dough until well kneaded, uniformly supple, and elastic. In a food processor, this takes about 40 seconds; in a mixer, it takes about 6 minutes. If mixing by hand, put ingredients in a large bowl and mix well.
6. Make a well in the center of the dough, pour in the yeast mixture, and work it into the ingredients. Then knead on a floured board until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes.
7. Transfer the dough to a large plastic bag, remove all air, and seal at the top. Place the dough in a bowl. Let rise in a warm spot until doubled, about 1 hour.
8. Oil a baking sheet. Punch the dough down and shape it into a smooth ball. Place the smooth side up on the baking sheet. Cover loosely with oiled plastic. Let rise in a warm spot until doubled, about 40-50 minutes.
9. Preheat the oven to 375F. Fifteen minutes before baking, put the rack in the center of the oven and dust the dough's top lightly with flour.
10. Make a decorative slash across the top. Bake until the bread is well browned and sounds hollow when tapped on the bottom, about 30-35 minutes. Immediately remove from the pan and cool on a rack. Makes 1 Loaf
